name: Olive Oil and Fennel Bread Sticks
recipeInstructions:
I love the taste of fennel and extra virgin olive oil.
Dissolve yeast in warm water and warm beer for 10 minutes, or until very foamy. Add olive oil, salt, and fennel seeds. Mix in 3 1/2 cups flour and knead until smooth and elastic, adding more flour as needed to prevent stickiness. Place dough in an oiled bowl, cover, and let rise double. Punch down dough and divide into 12 balls. Divide each ball into 4 pieces. Roll each piece into a long rope and place on greased baking sheets at least 1 inch apart. Brush with egg glaze.
Bake immediately at 350°F until golden, about 30 minutes. Note: I also like to sprinkle on rosemary, anise, or other seeds on the dough. Plus, I make my bread sticks larger by dividing the 12 balls of dough into 2 or 3 pieces instead of 4. And I also like to brush with extra virgin olive oil instead of egg wash over the breadsticks prior to baking them. Then turn them over during baking and brush more oil on them to brown the other side. Enjoy the taste of good olive oil!
